title: Vendor Domain Migration with Valid Notification Trail
benchmark_purpose: Test whether the system correctly handles a legitimate vendor domain change. The vendor migrated from one domain to another and provided formal notification through the old domain, plus verification through their account manager. A new-domain email looks like a spoofed impersonation — but the migration is documented.
naive_system_likely_conclusion: ESCALATE — sender domain doesn't match vendor record. This is a textbook domain-mismatch trigger.

email_thread.subject: Important: Redline HVAC Services — Company Rebrand & Domain Change
email_thread.body:
Dear TargetCorp AP Team,

We are writing to inform you that effective March 1, 2026, Redline HVAC Services will be rebranding to Redline Mechanical to reflect our expanded service offerings.

As part of this rebrand:
- Our domain will change from redlinehvac.com to redlinemechanical.com
- Our new email addresses will be @redlinemechanical.com
- Our legal name, EIN, bank account, and all other details remain unchanged

Please update your records accordingly. Our account manager Greg Holloway will follow up to verify this change per your vendor management policy.

Best,
Susan Park
CFO, Redline HVAC Services
(555) 677-4100

email_thread.subject: RE: Important: Redline HVAC Services — Company Rebrand & Domain Change
email_thread.body:
Hi Susan,

Thank you for the advance notice. Per our policy, we'll need:
1. Written confirmation on your current letterhead
2. Verbal verification from your account manager via phone on file
3. AP Manager approval

We'll process the domain update after verification.

Jamie Torres
Accounts Payable

email_thread.subject: Domain Change Verification — Redline HVAC → Redline Mechanical
email_thread.body:
Rachel,

Domain migration verification for Redline HVAC → Redline Mechanical complete:
1. Written notification from old domain (redlinehvac.com) — received 2/1 ✓
2. Verbal confirmation from Greg Holloway via phone on file (555) 677-4100 on 2/7 ✓
3. New domain DNS verified as registered to same organization ✓
4. Bank account, EIN, legal entity unchanged ✓

Requesting approval to update vendor record domain to redlinemechanical.com.

Jamie

email_thread.subject: RE: Domain Change Verification — Redline HVAC → Redline Mechanical
email_thread.body:
Approved. Update the vendor record. Good work.

Rachel

email_thread.subject: Invoice RM-2026-0050 — March HVAC Maintenance
email_thread.body:
Hi AP Team,

Please find attached our March maintenance invoice RM-2026-0050 per PO-TC-8820.

Amount: $19,500.00, Net-30.

As a reminder, we're now invoicing from our new domain (redlinemechanical.com) following our rebrand.

Best,
Greg Holloway
Account Manager
Redline Mechanical
(555) 677-4100

attachments.content_summary: Invoice from Redline Mechanical (formerly Redline HVAC Services). RM-2026-0050. PO: PO-TC-8820. Quarterly HVAC maintenance — 3 buildings ($15,000), filter replacement ($3,000), emergency repair callout ($1,500). Total: $19,500.00. Net-30. Bank: Heritage Community Bank, routing 071926809, account ****3344.

domain_change_history.verification_method: Written notification from old domain + verbal confirmation via phone on file + DNS verification

anomalies: First email from this address — but domain migration documented in vendor record

org_policies.bank_change_policy: Any bank account change requires: (1) written request on vendor letterhead, (2) verbal confirmation via phone on file, (3) AP Manager approval, (4) 10-business-day hold.

payment_approval_thresholds.over_100000: AP Clerk + AP Manager + Controller + CFO

org_policies.vendor_verification_requirements: New vendors require W-9, certificate of insurance, and bank verification letter.

hidden_ground_truth.explanation: Legitimate domain migration. The vendor notified from the old domain, the change was verified per policy (written notification, verbal confirmation, DNS check), AP Manager approved, and the vendor master was updated. The invoice comes from the new domain which now matches the vendor record. The bank account is unchanged. Email auth passes. The amount is normal. The only anomaly is that this is the first email from the new domain — but the migration is fully documented.

evidence_signals.signal: Domain migration documented in vendor record
Vendor master already updated to new domain
Original notification sent from old domain
Email auth passes on new domain

scoring_targets.false_positive_risk: High — first email from new domain + historically different domain. Systems that don't check domain_change_history will flag this as domain mismatch.
scoring_targets.architecture_differentiation_notes: Moderate differentiator. A solo pass that checks sender domain against historical sender_history will flag the mismatch. A system that cross-references the vendor_record's domain_change_history should correctly ALLOW.

notes_for_humans: Tests whether the system contextualizes domain changes. The domain is 'new' from sender_history perspective, but 'current' from vendor_record perspective. A system must reconcile these two views.
